reasoning: The asteroid 2024 YR4's estimated probability of impacting Earth reached a peak of around 3.1% to 3.2% (about 1 in 32) in mid-February 2025. Since then, additional observations have ruled out the impact, dropping the probability to effectively 0% (less than 0.001%) and lowering its Torino scale hazard to 0. Furthermore, NASA has noted that by mid-April 2025, the asteroid will be too far and faint to be observed by ground-based telescopes, meaning no new observational data will cause the impact probability to spike back up to 4% before the June 30, 2025 resolution date. Therefore, it is virtually impossible for the probability to hit the 4% threshold for an Earth impact. The current prediction market price of 2% aligns perfectly with this floor, reflecting near certainty of a 'No' resolution.
